question_title: Will Russia launch a single overnight aerial attack on Ukraine using more than 600 drones between July 9 and July 12, 2026?
summary: Threshold question on whether any of three nights (July 9-10, 10-11, 11-12) sees a Russian barrage exceeding 600 drones launched. In 2026 only 3 nights confirmed exceeded 600 (May 13-14: 675, Jun 1-2: 656, Jun 14-15: 611) out of ~60 nights ≈ 5%/night → ~14% over 3 nights. Escalation (record 8,150 drones in May, production ramping) and a current lull suggesting drone accumulation nudge upward, but 600+ nights are discrete clustered events and exact alignment with this specific 3-night window is uncertain. Sensitivity range 10-20%; settled slightly above pure base rate to reflect escalation.

factors.description: Base rate: 3 confirmed 600+ drone nights out of ~60 nights in May-June 2026 (~5%/night → ~14% over 3 nights)
Escalation trend: record 8,150 drones in May 2026 (+24% vs April), production ramping, so peak barrages may grow larger/more frequent
Current lull (late June/early July nights all 142-154 drones) suggests accumulation toward a large barrage, but timing alignment with window uncertain
600+ nights are discrete/clustered events (~every 2-3 weeks); the specific 3-night window may not align with a mega-barrage

reasoning:
**Forecast: 15%**

Russia exceeding 600 drones in a single overnight attack during July 9-12, 2026 resolves YES with ~15% probability. In 2026 only three nights exceeded 600 drones (May 13-14: 675, June 1-2: 656, June 14-15: 611) across ~60 nights, giving ~5%/night and ~14% over the 3-night window. Record-level production and a current lull (suggesting drone accumulation) push slightly upward, but these mega-barrages are discrete, clustered events whose alignment with this specific window is uncertain — keeping the estimate near the base rate.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, for at least one overnight Russian aerial attack whose Ukrainian Air Force morning summary is dated July 10, July 11, or July 12, 2026 (i.e., the nights of July 9-10, July 10-11, and July 11-12, 2026), the reported total number of drones (Shahed/Geran-type strike drones plus decoy drones such as Gerbera/Italmas) launched by Russia in that single overnight attack exceeds 600. The count of drones launched (not the number shot down) is what matters; missiles are not counted toward the 600 threshold. Resolution is based on figures published by the Ukrainian Air Force, as reported on Ukrinform's War section (https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato) or by other credible sources (https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). A single figure of '601 drones launched' would resolve YES.
fine_print: 'Drones launched' refers to the total number of strike and decoy unmanned aerial vehicles the Ukrainian Air Force reports Russia launched in that overnight attack, regardless of how many were shot down, jammed, or impacted. If the Air Force reports a combined total of 'air attack weapons' that lumps drones and missiles together without a separate drone figure, only the explicitly stated drone count is used; if no separate drone count is available for a given night, that night does not count toward YES. Only nights whose summaries are dated July 10, July 11, or July 12, 2026 are eligible; any night dated July 9 or earlier is excluded. If multiple eligible nights each exceed 600 drones, the question still resolves YES. If the Ukrainian Air Force does not publish overnight summaries for all three nights but no published eligible figure exceeds 600, the question resolves NO. The question resolves NO if no eligible overnight attack in the window exceeds 600 drones.

factors.description: Base rate: 3 confirmed 600+ drone nights (May 13-14: 675, Jun 1-2: 656, Jun 14-15: 611) out of ~60 nights in May-June 2026 = ~5%/night, giving ~14% over a 3-night window
Escalation trend: record 8,150 drones in May 2026 (+24% vs April), production ramping, so peak barrages may grow larger and more frequent
Current lull (late June/early July nights all 142-154 drones) suggests drone accumulation possibly building toward a large barrage, but timing alignment with July 9-12 window uncertain
600+ nights are discrete/clustered events (~every 2-3 weeks); exact 3-night window may not align with a mega-barrage

input.anchor: ~14% over 3 nights (3 of ~60 nights exceeded 600 drones in May-June 2026, per Ukrainian Air Force figures)
input.assessment: Threshold question on drone count over 3 nights (July 9-10, 10-11, 11-12). Modeled underlying quantity: ~5%/night for 600+ based on 3 events in ~60 nights. Over 3 nights ~14%. Escalation nudges up, but recent lull and clustering create timing uncertainty. Stable across sensitivity (10-20%).
input.tool_audit: research() returned excellent structured data with specific dates and drone counts. sandbox computed base rates. Both worked well.
input.process_reflection: Clean question for the research+compute pipeline. The research sub-agent correctly flagged direction-of-attack confounds (Ukrainian attacks ON Russia mislabeled), which was valuable. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors against the research trace and the corroborating worldview research entry (how-frequently-in-2026-has-russia-launched-overnig-8ca7fd6a.json). No errors found.

GROUNDING: Every factual claim is supported. The three 600+ nights (May 13-14: 675, Jun 1-2: 656, Jun 14-15: 611) and the lull nights (Jun 28: 142, Jun 30: 154, Jun 30-Jul 1: 151) are all present as research data points. The record 8,150 drones in May (+24% vs April) is sourced (Yahoo/AFP). No hallucination.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT (strong): The criteria require Ukrainian Air Force figures per Ukrinform, counting drones-launched (not downed), excluding missiles. The agent used exactly these sources, correctly separated drones from missiles, and — importantly — the research explicitly flagged and excluded the 660/992/555/556-drone figures that were Ukrainian attacks ON Russia (opposite direction). This is the key trap for this question type, and the agent navigated it correctly.

DIRECTIONS/DOUBLE-COUNTING: All logit signs match their descriptions (rare-event base rate negative; escalation positive; accumulation positive; timing-alignment risk negative). No shared-evidence double-counting; Factors 3 and 4 mildly overlap on timing but point opposite directions and net small.

PRE-PUBLICATION: None — the July 9-12 window is entirely forward of the July 1 publication date.

PROBABILITY: Math checks out — 3/60 = 5%/night, 1-(0.95)^3 = 14.3%, with small offsetting adjustments landing at 14%, essentially equal to the well-sourced anchor. My independent estimate agrees (~14-17%); no meaningful divergence and no unexplained anchor departure.

SELF-EXAMINATION: The counterargument is genuinely strong (coherent accumulation-phase story pushing to 20-25%, with concrete falsifiers), and 0.75 confidence is well-calibrated for a rare-event base-rate forecast with good data but real timing uncertainty.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent's narrative ("both worked well," no failures) matches ground truth (3 calls, 0 errors).

No cross-question or worldview contradictions found; the only related worldview entry is this session's own research, which is consistent with the factors.
